- grep_code: Search the codebase for a regex pattern (ripgrep/grep). Use to find symbols, imports, usages, and conventions before editing.
- glob_files: Find files by glob pattern (e.g. **/*.vue, src/**/*.ts). Use before read_file to locate paths.
- semantic_search: Hybrid semantic + keyword codebase search (retriv: BM25 + local embeddings). Finds relevant code by meaning — e.g. 'authentication flow', 'chat state store', 'verify before handoff'. Use alongside grep_code (exact symbols) and glob_files (file patterns). Auto-indexes on first use.
- list_directory: List files and directories in a workspace path.
- read_file: Read exact source lines (numbered). Secondary to inspect_ast — use only when AST output is insufficient or you need precise text before search_replace. - read_files: Batch read multiple files (numbered lines). Up to 10 paths. Prefer inspect_ast per file for structure Q&A — batch read only when exact source is needed for edits. Do not batch-read files already covered by inspect_ast this turn.
- check_syntax: Parse a source file with tree-sitter to detect syntax errors and Vue/HTML markup issues (missing </template>, </style>, etc.). Call after write_file on code files before verify/build.
- read_lints: Run project-native linters/typecheckers scoped to a file or directory (eslint, vue-tsc, tsc, ruff, cargo check, go vet). Falls back gracefully when no linter is configured — use check_syntax for universal parse checks.
- write_file: Create or overwrite a file in the workspace. Parent directories are created automatically. If STATUS: SKIP (already exists), use search_replace to edit or mark_plan_step done — do not retry write_file.
- search_replace: Apply a surgical edit: replace old_string with new_string in an existing file. Prefer this over write_file for small changes. old_string must match exactly once unless replace_all is true.
- run_bash: Execute a non-interactive bash command in the workspace. NEVER use interactive scaffolders (npm create vue@latest, vue create, npm init without -y). NEVER chain npm run dev — dev servers are blocked; use run_check or verify_project for build/lint/test. For new projects: npm create vite@latest <dir> -- --template vue, or write_file package.json + entry files then npm install. For verification steps prefer run_check (structured label + pass/fail). Returns stdout/stderr.
- run_check: Run a named verification command (build, lint, test) with structured pass/fail output. Prefer this over run_bash for verification steps — the label appears in the activity timeline.
- verify_project: Verify a project folder. Manifest projects (package.json, Cargo.toml, …): runs discovered lint/test/build scripts. Bare projects (HTML/JS/CSS only, no manifest): runs check_syntax on sources. REQUIRED: pass path to the project folder — not workspace root ".". Example: {"path":"my-site"}. Returns OVERALL: PASS or OVERALL: FAIL.
- delete_file: Delete a file from the workspace. Use to remove wrong scaffolds or stray files. Cannot delete .git, .jarvis, or node_modules paths.
- web_search: Search the web via SearXNG for current documentation or facts. Top hits include fetched page body text (not just snippets) for deep research. When answering from results, cite source URLs for every excerpt. Use ONE comprehensive query per task — avoid repeat searches in the same turn. Pick one or more comma-separated SearXNG engines via the optional `engines` argument to vary results and avoid repeated blocks. Rotate engines when retrying a weak search. Allowed engines: google (broad general web); bing (general web, Microsoft index); brave (privacy-focused general web); duckduckgo (alternative general index); startpage (Google results via proxy); qwant (EU-focused general web); mojeek (independent crawler).
- recall_brain: Search Jarvis core memory and learned skills on demand (keyword + semantic hybrid). Scoped to the active project plus global items by default. Use when a task may depend on past fixes, project facts, preferences, or workflows that are not in the pinned brain index.
- remember: Save a lasting fact, preference, project detail, issue fix, or workflow to Jarvis core memory. Auto-tags the active project unless project is set to global. Use importance 5 + category fix for bug/issue fixes; 4–5 for critical project notes.
- learn_skill: Save a reusable skill or workflow pattern Jarvis should follow in future tasks. Auto-tags the active project unless project is global.
- apply_template: Copy bundled code templates into a project directory. Use after scaffolding or when user wants boilerplate UI.
- cleanup_stray_paths: Remove mistaken folders/files at workspace root (e.g. stray src/ outside the active project). Only deletes paths flagged as strays — safe after scaffold mistakes.

Steps must implement the injected acceptance criteria; omit work already satisfied (EXISTS/SKIP). Each step: { id, label, status? } where status is pending|in_progress|done|skipped. First step is auto-set to in_progress. Include a final verify step.
